faqs.question: How do I know if I have carpenter ants in my Williams Bridge home?
faqs.answer: Signs include piles of wood shavings (frass) near baseboards or windowsills, rustling sounds in walls, and seeing large black ants especially during spring evenings. Carpenter ants are typically active at night and are larger than common household ants. If you notice winged ants indoors, this often indicates an established colony requiring professional treatment.

faqs.question: What factors affect the cost of carpenter ant control in Williams Bridge?
faqs.answer: Pricing ranges from $350 for minor infestations in small apartments to $2,000 for severe infestations in larger homes. Factors include the size of your property, number of colonies present, extent of structural damage, accessibility of nesting sites, and whether follow-up treatments are needed. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Are your carpenter ant treatments safe for children and pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products and application methods that are safe for your family and pets when applied by our licensed technicians.
